issues.title: Client certificates / CertificateRequest (Section 3.1)
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### CertificateRequest optional (Section 3.1)

Backlog note: may be removed along with client cert caching.

- [ ] **Question:** Server MAY send empty extension in CR — do clients need it to send CT extension?
- [ ] **Current understanding:** Yes per draft (client MUST NOT send CT extension unless server sent CR extension)
- [ ] **Action:** Verify and document

**Context (EKR):** The client/server setting is not symmetrical: the server often does not know the client identity in advance, so caching semantics for client certificates need explicit treatment (see discussion in this issue history).

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

comments.author: yaronf

issues.title: Introduction: security threat + cite EKR (informative reference)
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Introduction (draft) — motivation for readers

**Security threat.** A cryptanalytic break or emergency affecting classical signature schemes can leave previously issued TLS (and PKI) credentials vulnerable to a capable adversary. Deployments may need to migrate authentication to post-quantum algorithms quickly and to **avoid downgrade** to broken classical algorithms for peers that have already accepted a **commitment** to PQC. That “emergency migration” framing—why rollback is dangerous once clients have cached a restriction—is a core motivation for PQC Continuity.

**Informative reference:** Eric Rescorla’s [Educated Guesswork — PQ emergency](https://educatedguesswork.org/posts/pq-emergency/) gives an accessible, informal description of this threat and response pattern.

### Draft action

- [ ] Add citation in draft (Introduction or informative references).

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: Certificate chains: mixed chains; align draft with strict POC policy
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Draft gaps (POC tests A3, A5)

| Test | Scenario | Implied gap |
|------|----------|-------------|
| A3 | PQC leaf + RSA intermediate (mixed chain) | Draft does not specify behavior for mixed chains |
| A5 | Mixed chain (PQC leaf + traditional CA) | Draft does not specify behavior for mixed chains |

- [ ] **A3, A5:** Add normative text on mixed chains (PQC leaf + traditional CA/intermediate).

### Clarifications (Section 3.1)

- [ ] **Question:** PQC leaf + traditional intermediate — does draft imply leaf-only PQC?
  - **POC policy (implemented):** If any certificate in the full verified chain (EE, intermediates, root) is non-PQC, abort the handshake. A non-PQC intermediate or root breaks quantum-resistance and could indicate a CRQC attack on a CA.
  - **Open:** Should the draft mandate this explicitly? The current text only says the EE cert must be PQC; it is silent on intermediates and roots.

### Proposal

When PQC Continuity applies, the draft should be **strict**: mixed chains (e.g. PQC EE with a non-PQC issuer chain, or any non-PQC cert in the validated chain) **MUST** be rejected with a specified alert—aligned with the POC—rather than staying silent.

**Related:** Mention in prose that for PQ resistance the entire chain should be PQ-signed even if the draft cannot always mandate it in all deployments.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

comments.author: yaronf
comments.body:
**Current POC behavior** ([pqc-continuity-poc](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc)): after path validation, if **any** certificate in the verified chain (EE, intermediates, trust anchor) is not treated as PQC, the implementation **aborts the handshake**. Rationale: a traditional intermediate or root breaks end-to-end post-quantum resistance for the commitment and may indicate a mis-issued or attacked chain.

**Proposal for the draft:** When PQC Continuity applies (peer has sent / is bound by the CT extension and caching rules), the document should be **strict**: **mixed chains** (e.g. PQC EE with a non-PQC issuer chain, or any non-PQC cert in the validated chain) MUST be rejected with a specified alert—aligned with this POC policy—rather than staying silent or informational only. This closes the A3/A5 gaps relative to the POC test plan (mixed-chain scenarios).

issues.title: CDNs and middleboxes
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### CDNs and middleboxes (draft section)

- [ ] Add draft text on **CDN provisioning** (different chains for the same origin), **middlebox** behavior, and how commitments relate to **origin identity** rather than a specific presented chain.

**Notes (from earlier issue text):** CDNs are provisioned by the origin; different CDNs may present different chains for the same server. Middleboxes that must not break traffic should not commit spuriously; breakage can occur if a middlebox is a PQC client but not a PQC server while the origin has already committed. Commitments are keyed per origin (e.g. DNS name), not the entire chain, because an attacker may replace the chain.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

comments.author: yaronf
comments.body: The most important point is that recovery is super-easy - just get a PQC cert for the server and install it. This would recover everybody other than clients that are behind a misbehaving middlebox.

issues.title: Appendix: design alternatives (trust anchors, HSTS, etc.)
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Appendix: Design alternatives (draft)

- [ ] Optional draft **appendix**: compare PQC Continuity to other approaches (e.g. trust anchors in DNS, HSTS-style patterns, layering tradeoffs).

**Ideas to cover (informative):**

- **Trust anchors:** DNS often owned separately from server operators; trust-anchor records do not pin a single algorithm family; clients may still accept certs under unpublished local anchors (rollback remains possible).
- **HSTS:** HTTP-specific; pinning/middlebox breakage concerns; app layer may need exact PQ algorithm lists; origin takeover can “brick” similarly; cache-poisoning classes differ (e.g. client-side attacks).

Use this issue for scratch notes; final text belongs in the draft appendix.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: Wire format: drop `signature_algorithm` from CT extension
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Drop `signature_algorithm` from CT extension

- [ ] **Proposal:** Remove `signature_algorithm` (2 bytes) from the Certificate extension body, leaving only `algorithm_validity_period` (4 bytes). Full analysis: [pqc-continuity-poc `docs/analysis-drop-algorithm-field.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/analysis-drop-algorithm-field.md).

- [ ] **Rationale:** Per-algorithm pinning is not enforceable against an active attacker — the attacker can steer TLS negotiation to any algorithm the client advertises, regardless of the cached value. The integrity check (A1/A2/P5) is real but narrow: it detects a misbehaving server, not the CRQC attacker the draft is designed against. The registry is still required for the "is cert PQC?" gating check; dropping the field removes scheme-matching from the parse path but not the PQC-cert check itself.

- [ ] **Impact if adopted:** Extension shrinks from 6 to 4 bytes. A1/A2/P5 error categories disappear. PQC→PQC server migration is free (no cache update needed). Mixed-chain and empty-body semantics are unaffected.

- [ ] **Action:** Propose in draft; if accepted, update POC implementation (`poc-backlog.md`).

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: Malformed extension data (P4); normative handling
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Draft gap — POC test P4

| Test | Scenario | Implied gap |
|------|----------|-------------|
| P4 | Malformed extension data (wrong length) | Draft does not specify: reject handshake or ignore? |

### Draft action

- [ ] Add normative text on malformed extension handling.

**POC / implementation direction:** When the CT extension data is malformed, the client **must abort** the handshake (e.g. `decode_error` per TLS 1.3 parsing rules where appropriate). The draft must not leave “ignore” as an option for on-the-wire garbage.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: Normative validation: P5, EE `signature_algorithm` mismatch, CT placement
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Draft gap — POC test P5

| Test | Scenario | Implied gap |
|------|----------|-------------|
| P5 | Unknown SignatureScheme in extension | Draft says recipient must validate; does not say abort — POC aborts with `illegal_parameter` |

- [ ] **P5:** Add explicit requirement to **reject** unknown/invalid `SignatureScheme` (e.g. `illegal_parameter`).

### Signature algorithm mismatch (Section 3.1)

- [ ] **Question:** `signature_algorithm` does not match cert — MUST NOT cache or reject handshake?
  - Current draft (§3.3) implies validation failure → don't cache, but does not mandate abort.
  - **POC:** Extension scheme must exactly match the EE cert public key; wrong PQC alg, legacy alg, or unknown scheme → **`illegal_parameter`** (RFC 8446 §6.2).
  - **Action:** Draft **MUST** abort with `illegal_parameter` if `signature_algorithm` does not match the EE cert's public key.

### CT extension placement (Section 3.1)

- [ ] If the CT extension appears on any `CertificateEntry` other than the first (EE), recipients **MUST** abort with **`illegal_parameter`**.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: Cache key (Section 3.3): hostname, port, transport; ALPN; RFC 9525
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
> OPEN ISSUE: do we discuss how the cache is indexed? Service identity per RFC 9525?

- [ ] **Question:** Exact format for cache key? Hostname only? Hostname + port? RFC 9525 full service identity?
- [ ] **Question:** IP vs hostname — same server, different forms?
- [ ] **Transport dimension:** `(hostname, port)` is ambiguous across transports (TLS/TCP vs DTLS/UDP). Proposed minimum key: **`(hostname, port, transport)`**.
- [ ] **ALPN dimension:** Optional fourth key field for different certificate policies on the same endpoint.
- [ ] **Action:** Update draft: `(hostname, port, transport)` as minimum **MUST**; ALPN optional.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: Section 3.2: SHOULD vs MUST for cached `signature_algorithms`
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Ambiguity

- [ ] **Question:** "SHOULD include the cached algorithm" — what if client omits it but sends other PQC algs?
- [ ] **Impact:** Server behavior when client sends only non-cached PQC algorithms
- [ ] **Action:** Test and report; consider upgrading to **MUST** where a cache hit applies.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

comments.author: yaronf
comments.body: Superseded by #9: the extension carries only algorithm_validity_period, not SignatureScheme. No normative pin to a specific scheme on cache hit; enforcement is PQC EE cert and policy. The old SHOULD-vs-MUST-for-recorded-scheme work does not apply.

issues.title: Remove “few seconds’ tolerance” language (Section 3.3)
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Tolerance for validity decrease (Section 3.3)

- [ ] **Draft change:** **Remove** the vestigial “within a few seconds’ tolerance” wording (no specified value, little practical effect for month-scale validity).

**POC:** Silently keeps the cached (longer) expiry on any decrease — conservative behavior.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: `algorithm_validity_period == 0`: semantics + operational stale-cache risk
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Zero period semantics (Section 3.1, 3.3)

- [ ] **Question:** Sender with `algorithm_validity_period == 0`: commitment or opt-out?
- [ ] **Action:** Clarify; test both interpretations

### Zero validity: operational risk (Section 3.3)

- [ ] **Gap:** Operators switching to **0** after advertising positive validity: clients that already cached may stay PQC-only until prior expiry (months/years). Validity **0** is not instant global revocation.
- [ ] **Action:** Operational considerations: operators must wait at least `max(previously_advertised_validity)` before assuming all clients cleared cache.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

issues.title: Composite `signature_algorithm`: definition + LAMPS composite draft
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Composite certs

- [ ] **Question:** How is "composite" defined for `signature_algorithm`? IANA / implementation values?
- [ ] **Action:** Check IANA TLS SignatureScheme registry; OpenSSL composite support; cite **[draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs/)** (LAMPS composite ML-DSA for PKIX).

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

comments.author: yaronf
comments.body:
Scope update (post GitHub #9): the Certificate extension no longer carries a `SignatureScheme`; PQC vs traditional is determined only from the EE certificate.

For this issue, treat **PQC** as one bucket: **pure-PQ** EE certs and **composite-PQ** EE certs (per {{?I-D.ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs}}—composite is a **single** signature algorithm at the protocol/PKIX layer, not per-component policy mapping). The draft should cite that draft for composite and avoid implying fine-grained mapping of certificate components to separate TLS schemes.

Normative work here is clarifying that single coarse classification for enforcement, not IANA/wire `SignatureScheme` in the extension.

issues.title: Security Considerations (Section 4) + cache-churn / DoS
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
### Security Considerations

- [ ] **Draft:** Section 4 TODO — first-connection trust, cache poisoning, related threats.

### Cache churn / DoS via commitment changes

- [ ] **Issue:** Malicious or compromised server repeatedly changes commitment → client updates persistent cache every handshake → I/O DoS on client.
- [ ] **Action:** Rate-limiting / conservative cache updates, or at minimum call out in Security Considerations.

---
**This issue is the source of truth** for this topic. **POC working copy:** [`docs/draft-backlog.md`](https://github.com/yaronf/pqc-continuity-poc/blob/main/docs/draft-backlog.md) in pqc-continuity-poc — update that file when this issue changes.

reviews.author: yaronf
reviews.body: Looks good, other than one comment.

comments.body: I prefer the original text here, because a client may connect through a MITM for a few times, but once it connects directly even once, it obtains correct information.

pulls.title: Language consistency improvements
pulls.author: yaronf
pulls.body:
- Standardize terminology: use "PQC" consistently instead of "PQ"
- Standardize MitM capitalization (was "MiTM")
- Fix field name references: use `algorithm_validity_period` consistently
- Add code formatting to `pq_cert_available` identifier
- Fix RFC 2119 keyword: change "should" to "SHOULD"
- Add missing comma in abstract after "TLS layer"
- Improve clarity: "this extension" instead of "the extension"
- Complete IANA section: add "registry." to request sentence
- Fix IANA table spacing: "CH, CR, CT" (was "CH, CR,CT")

pulls.title: Technical consistency improvements
pulls.author: yaronf
pulls.body:
## Summary

This PR addresses technical consistency issues identified in the draft:

- **Bidirectional scope clarification**: Abstract and introduction now explicitly state the mechanism works peer-to-peer, with typical use being client-caching-server but also supporting server-caching-client
- **Cache update logic**: Clarified that when switching algorithms, use the new validity period from the extension
- **Zero value semantics**: Defined that `algorithm_validity_period` of zero means don't cache (if no cache exists) or clear cache (if exists)
- **Validation requirements**: Specified that `signature_algorithm` must match certificate and must be a PQC algorithm
- **Extension presence logic**: Clarified that when using traditional (non-PQC) certificate, sender should send extension with no data to indicate support
- **Commitment timing**: Clarified that duration is measured from the current TLS handshake

## Changes

All changes improve clarity and consistency without changing the fundamental protocol design.

pulls.title: PQC continuity: all normative changes derived from implementation
pulls.author: yaronf
pulls.body:
Implements GitHub issues #9, #11, #12, #13, #15, #16, #17 (and related cleanup). See commit messages for per-issue breakdown.

Closes #9, #11, #12, #13, #15, #16, #17.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
